title: Package HELIX as standards-compliant Agent Skills library
description: Repackage HELIX as a portable Agent Skills library layered under the HELIX workflow and CLI without weakening the method. Preserve the core HELIX contract: command mirroring, authority-ordered planning, bounded actions, tracker-first execution, and workflow docs as the normative source of behavior. Scope includes directory/name alignment, canonical .agents/skills packaging, standards-oriented metadata and validation, and a docs rewrite that presents skills as the portable surface and the HELIX workflow/CLI as the stricter operating layer.
design: Deliver in four ordered slices. First, rename skill directories so published names, on-disk layout, and installer references match exactly. Second, adopt project-level and user-level .agents/skills packaging as the canonical layout, with .claude/skills retained only as a temporary compatibility mirror. Third, add the minimum metadata and deterministic validation needed to keep the package internally consistent. Fourth, rewrite the docs so the portable skill surface is clear while deeper HELIX semantics remain in the workflow contract.
acceptance: All child issues are closed. Every published skill directory basename matches its public skill name. Canonical project/user .agents/skills packaging is implemented and documented. Skill metadata and deterministic validation exist and are documented. README, AGENTS, and workflow docs consistently present HELIX as portable skills plus a stricter workflow/CLI layer. Relevant tests and diff-check pass.

title: Rename HELIX skill directories to match published skill names
description: Rename the HELIX skill directories so each directory basename exactly matches the public skill name declared in SKILL.md and documented in the CLI/skills contract. This work should eliminate layout drift between the published interface, installer targets, and the repository tree before any packaging or validation changes build on top of it.
design: Do this first so later packaging and validation work can depend on a stable layout. Rename the directories in-place, update all references in the installer, docs, and tests, and do not keep legacy alias directories. Preserve the existing published skill set and command mirroring semantics.
acceptance: Every published skill directory basename matches the SKILL.md name field exactly. No stale references to pre-rename directory paths remain in installer scripts, docs, or tests. The published skill set and command mapping are unchanged. Relevant tests and diff-check pass.

title: Adopt project-level .agents/skills packaging for HELIX
description: Make .agents/skills the canonical packaging surface for HELIX at both project and user scope. The repo should present agent-compatible skills through .agents/skills while .claude/skills remains only a temporary compatibility mirror until Claude documents .agents support directly.
design: Build on the renamed directory layout. Define the project-level and user-level .agents/skills conventions, update installation logic to populate the canonical .agents path, and keep .claude mirroring strictly as a compatibility layer. Remove any remaining documentation that treats client-native paths as canonical.
acceptance: Project-level and user-level .agents/skills packaging is implemented or explicitly defined as the canonical layout. Installer behavior populates .agents and mirrors to .claude only where compatibility requires it. Docs no longer describe client-native paths as canonical. Relevant tests and diff-check pass.

title: Add Agent Skills metadata and validation for HELIX skills
description: Add the minimum standards-oriented metadata and deterministic validation needed to keep the HELIX skill package internally consistent once naming and packaging are stable. The goal is a small, enforceable contract rather than speculative metadata bloat.
design: After directory names and .agents packaging settle, define the required metadata fields and add a deterministic validation command or harness that enforces directory-name to skill-name alignment, required frontmatter, and the chosen packaging invariants. Document the validation path alongside the packaging contract.
acceptance: Required metadata fields are explicitly defined and present where needed. A deterministic validation command or harness checks directory-name versus skill-name alignment, required frontmatter, and chosen packaging rules. Validation is documented in the repo. Relevant tests, validation, and diff-check pass.

title: Rewrite HELIX docs around portable skills plus workflow contract
description: Rewrite the public HELIX docs so they describe two layers cleanly: a portable skill library surface and a stricter HELIX workflow/CLI contract. The rewrite must remove legacy naming, path drift, and agent-specific invocation conventions that are no longer part of the public interface.
design: Do this last, after packaging and validation are true. Keep the portable skill story concise and standards-oriented in the top-level docs, and keep deeper operational semantics in the workflow contract. Ensure the docs reinforce that public skill names mirror CLI commands exactly and that .agents is canonical while .claude is temporary compatibility.
acceptance: README, AGENTS, and workflow docs consistently describe HELIX as portable skills plus a stricter workflow/CLI layer. Portable packaging guidance is clearly separated from HELIX-specific execution semantics. .agents is documented as canonical and .claude as temporary compatibility. No stale naming, path, or slash-command guidance remains. Relevant tests and diff-check pass.

title: Harden HELIX tracker against races and JSONL corruption
description: Strengthen the built-in HELIX tracker so concurrent or interrupted updates do not silently corrupt .helix/issues.jsonl, and so failure modes are explicitly detected, surfaced, and tested. Scope includes write-path hardening, conflict detection, corruption handling, and deterministic race/corruption verification.
design: Follow the tracker write-safety ADR and tracker mutation contract first, then implement guarded mutations and deterministic tests before expanding metadata mutation surfaces. Prefer additive safeguards over ad hoc recovery logic.
acceptance: Tracker writes detect or prevent lost updates and malformed JSONL corruption under the supported local execution model. Corruption and conflict handling are explicit and test-covered. Deterministic tests exercise concurrent update, interrupted write, malformed file, and recovery/reporting behavior. HELIX docs and skill guidance describe the tracker safety contract accurately.
notes: 2026-03-27 verification: reviewed ADR-002, API-001, TD-002, workflows/TRACKER.md, scripts/tracker.sh, and tests/helix-cli.sh coverage for lock serialization, atomic rewrites, malformed JSONL failure, and queue-drift-safe execution. Canonical proof lane: bash tests/helix-cli.sh exited 0 with PASS: 68 helix wrapper tests. git diff --check exited 0. Remaining tracker metadata work is tracked separately in hx-72f06ccb and is outside this epic's acceptance scope.

title: Specify tracker concurrency and corruption-handling contract
description: Accidental malformed tracker issue created during planning. Superseded by hx-1044d5dc.
design: Do not use this issue for implementation planning.
acceptance: Superseded issue remains non-actionable and documented so tracker history is explicit.
notes: Accidental malformed tracker issue created during planning; superseded by hx-1044d5dc.

title: Specify tracker concurrency and corruption-handling contract
description: Define the supported tracker mutation surface, write model, conflict detection expectations, corruption detection and handling, and how interrupted or concurrent mutations are surfaced to operators.
design: Land the tracker write-safety ADR and the tracker mutation contract first. Clarify what guarantees the built-in JSONL tracker does and does not make. Define the minimum acceptable safety properties before implementation: atomic replacement behavior, malformed-file detection, conflict visibility, conservative failure behavior, and the mutation surfaces required by HELIX refinement flows.
acceptance: ADR-002 and API-001 define tracker mutation safety, corruption handling, conflict/reporting behavior, and required metadata mutation APIs clearly enough to drive tests and implementation. The contract names the deterministic verification expected for tracker write-path changes.

title: Add deterministic tracker race and corruption test coverage
description: Extend deterministic tests to cover concurrent or conflicting tracker mutations, malformed issues.jsonl input, interrupted write scenarios, and conservative failure behavior.
design: Test the tracker as a stateful file-backed system against ADR-002, API-001, and the supervisory-concurrency plan. Cover malformed issues.jsonl input, failed mutation attempts on malformed state, lock timeout reporting, and concurrent local mutation scenarios that must fail closed instead of corrupting tracker state. Favor deterministic harness orchestration over probabilistic stress tests.
acceptance: tests/helix-cli.sh reproduces malformed issues.jsonl read or mutation failure, lock-timeout failure reporting, and concurrent local mutation safety behavior under the supported local execution model. The harness fails if tracker commands silently continue on malformed state or write partial state. bash tests/helix-cli.sh and git diff --check pass.

title: Harden tracker write paths and metadata mutation APIs
description: Implement the tracker-side safeguards needed to make JSONL mutations safe enough for HELIX use, including guarded writes, explicit malformed-file handling, and first-class metadata update support needed by refinement workflows.
design: Implement the tracker mutation contract from API-001 under the write-safety model from ADR-002. Introduce the smallest sufficient write-path hardening that prevents silent corruption and lost updates in the supported execution model. Extend tracker mutations through the CLI instead of relying on direct file edits for refs, deps, parent, and related metadata where needed.
acceptance: scripts/tracker.sh and the tracker CLI prevent or detect unsafe concurrent or interrupted updates under the supported model, surface malformed tracker state explicitly, and expose the metadata mutation operations required by HELIX refinement flows. Deterministic tests and docs are updated with the new behavior.

title: Teach helix run to revalidate queue drift before claim and close
description: Governing artifacts: PRD, FEAT-001, ADR-001, ADR-002, SD-001, TD-002, API-001, TP-002, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-03-27-supervisory-concurrency.md. Update the bounded run loop so a live helix run session re-reads the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close, detects material queue drift caused by concurrent local refinement, and refuses stale claim/close behavior.
design: Build on the tracker safety contract instead of inventing hidden runner state. Define material drift from tracker metadata, stop or re-check conservatively when the selected issue changed, and keep direct interactive refinement compatible with a live automated session.
acceptance: helix run revalidates a selected issue before claim and before close. Deterministic tests cover issue drift before claim and before close. Concurrent local refinement causes a safe re-check or stop instead of stale claim/close behavior.
notes: Partial progress landed: supervisory concurrency specs are updated, tracker structural mutation APIs and malformed-state safeguards are implemented, and the implementation action/prompt now requires pre-claim and pre-close revalidation. Remaining work: make the live run path enforce queue-drift revalidation behavior deterministically.

title: Add tracker supersession and execution-eligibility metadata for live supervision
description: Governing artifacts: PRD, FEAT-001, ADR-001, ADR-002, SD-001, TD-002, API-001, TP-002, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-03-27-supervisory-concurrency.md. Extend the tracker mutation surface and runner contract with first-class supersession/replacement and execution-eligibility metadata so concurrent local refinement can replace or disqualify execution work without ad hoc heuristics.
design: Build on the structural mutation APIs and queue-drift revalidation already landed. Add the smallest explicit metadata needed for live supervision: execution-safe eligibility for run selection and supersession/replacement relationships for refined work. Update the harness to prove the runner honors those signals.
acceptance: The tracker exposes first-class mutation/query support for execution eligibility and supersession or replacement relationships. helix run excludes non-executable refinement work from implementation selection and re-checks or stops when an issue is superseded. Deterministic tests cover both behaviors.

title: --help
Update quickstart demo cast to canonical HELIX skill names
description: Fresh-eyes review of hx-7c6f43f4 found that docs/demos/helix-quickstart/recordings/helix-quickstart-20260319-175130.cast still renders legacy skill names in the skill table (for example helix and helix-alignment-review) even though the published contract requires helix-<command> names and the rename issue acceptance required stale docs references to be removed.
design: Keep the demo recording aligned with the published HELIX skill contract. Update the recording or regenerate the affected portion so the visible skill list and path output use canonical names consistently.
acceptance: The demo cast no longer shows legacy published skill names or pre-rename skill paths. Documentation examples stay consistent with README.md and workflow skill naming rules.

title: Backfill AGENTS for skill validator contract
description: Fresh-eyes review of hx-f2ff3220 found that AGENTS.md was not updated to document the new deterministic skill-package validator, metadata requirements, and when to run the validator.

title: Remove stale agent-specific automation references from workflow docs
description: Fresh-eyes review of hx-33b29b5c found remaining public-doc references to deprecated agent-specific loop recipes after the skill-packaging rewrite. Evidence: workflows/README.md:206 still says EXECUTION.md contains autonomous Codex or Claude loop recipes, but EXECUTION.md no longer documents them; workflows/TRACKER.md:393 still points readers to Codex or Claude automation patterns. Update the workflow docs so public guidance consistently describes the portable skill layer and HELIX workflow contract without stale agent-specific invocation language.

title: Restore .claude directory creation in quickstart demo
description: Fresh-eyes review of last commit 6291a3e found a regression in docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh after the .agents packaging update. The script now creates .agents and copies skills there, but it still redirects to .claude/settings.json without first creating .claude. On a clean workspace this fails immediately with: zsh: no such file or directory: .claude/settings.json. Evidence: docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh:144-146 and a clean tmpdir reproduction.
design: Keep the canonical .agents skill install path, but preserve the demo's Claude settings write by creating .claude before redirecting to .claude/settings.json, or by updating the demo flow so the settings file path is created safely.
acceptance: Running the quickstart demo setup in a clean workspace no longer fails when writing Claude settings. The script consistently creates any required parent directories before redirecting files. The demo recording remains aligned with the actual script behavior.

title: HELIX alignment review: repo
description: Repo-wide HELIX alignment review for workflow contract, planning stack, implementation, tests, and packaging. Governing action: workflows/actions/reconcile-alignment.md. Scope: repo.
design: Review top-down against the HELIX authority stack. Reuse existing issues where possible, create one review task per functional area, consolidate findings in a durable alignment report, then generate deterministic execution issues for uncovered gaps only.
acceptance: One durable alignment report exists for repo scope. Review issues cover each functional area. Every non-aligned gap is classified with planning and implementation evidence, has a resolution direction, and is either covered by execution issues or explicitly deferred with rationale.

title: Review repo planning stack and workflow contract traceability
description: Review vision, PRD, feature specs, design artifacts, test plans, and workflow contract docs for repo scope. Identify missing links, contradictions, stale artifacts, and implementation-plan gaps without using source code to invent intent.
design: Compare only canonical planning artifacts first, then map downstream docs and tests to the authority order.
acceptance: All repo planning artifacts are classified with evidence and any non-aligned planning-stack gaps are recorded in the alignment report.

title: Review helix run and wrapper CLI against governing artifacts
description: Review scripts/helix, workflow prompts, and deterministic loop behavior against PRD, FEAT-001, FEAT-002, SD-001, TD-002, and TP-002 for repo scope.
design: Trace documented acceptance criteria to wrapper code paths and deterministic tests, with special attention to queue-drain control, review handling, and concurrent refinement revalidation.
acceptance: Wrapper-related gaps are classified with planning evidence, code/test evidence, and deterministic follow-up work where needed.

title: Review HELIX skill packaging, installer, and demo surfaces
description: Review skills/, .agents/skills packaging, scripts/install-local-skills.sh, tests/validate-skills.sh, and demo/operator-facing installation surfaces for repo scope.
design: Validate published skill names, metadata, package-relative links, installer behavior, and visible demo flows against the packaging contract.
acceptance: Packaging and installer gaps are classified with evidence, and demo-facing regressions are accounted for in the alignment report and tracker coverage.

title: Review built-in tracker implementation and safety contract
description: Review workflows/TRACKER.md, ADR-002, API-001, scripts/tracker.sh, and tracker coverage in tests for repo scope.
design: Validate tracker command semantics, write-safety behavior, and mutation surfaces against the current contract without assuming undocumented guarantees.
acceptance: Tracker-related gaps are classified with explicit evidence and mapped to execution issues where follow-up remains open.

title: Implement supervisory plan/polish routing in helix check and run
description: After the workflow contract is reconciled, update scripts/helix, check prompt handling, and deterministic tests so helix-run can route to plan and polish when governing artifacts or open work require those bounded actions, instead of limiting NEXT_ACTION to IMPLEMENT, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, and STOP.
design: Keep execution bounded and least-power. Extend the NEXT_ACTION contract and run-loop dispatch only after the governing docs are explicit. Add deterministic tests proving functionality-change and spec-change scenarios route to plan/polish before implementation resumes, without regressing existing ALIGN/BACKFILL/WAIT/GUIDANCE behavior.
acceptance: helix check and helix run can represent and dispatch the reconciled supervisory action set. Deterministic tests cover plan and polish routing from representative repo states. Existing queue-drain and review behavior continues to pass.

title: Create canonical HELIX implementation plan artifact
description: The HELIX authority stack names docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md as the canonical implementation-plan layer, but that artifact is currently absent in this repository. Create the durable implementation plan that maps active HELIX execution work to governing requirements, designs, tests, and execution ordering.
design: Keep the implementation plan repo-scoped and tracker-first. Trace current open build work and near-term execution slices back to the governing PRD, feature specs, solution/technical designs, and test plans so downstream implementation no longer skips the documented plan layer.
acceptance: docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md exists and traces current execution slices to governing artifacts, dependencies, and verification expectations. Open implementation issues cite the plan where appropriate.

title: Reconcile workflow contract with supervisory plan/polish dispatch
description: Higher-order HELIX artifacts now define helix-run as a supervisory controller that can route to align, plan, polish, implement, review, check, and backfill, but workflows/README.md still states that plan and polish do not participate in check -> NEXT_ACTION dispatch. Reconcile the normative workflow contract so README, EXECUTION, and check semantics match the PRD, ADR-001, and SD-001 before more runtime work lands.
design: Update the lowest-authority conflicting workflow contract docs to match the governing PRD/ADR/SD decisions. Be explicit about which actions participate in supervisory dispatch, which remain operator-only, and what NEXT_ACTION vocabulary the loop owns after reconciliation.
acceptance: The normative workflow contract no longer contradicts the supervisory control design. workflows/README.md, workflows/EXECUTION.md, and workflows/actions/check.md consistently describe whether and how plan and polish participate in supervisory dispatch. The resulting contract is specific enough to drive runtime and test updates without ambiguity.

title: Honor machine-readable review findings in helix run
description: Governing artifacts: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md. FEAT-002 and TD-002 require post-implementation review findings to be surfaced as actionable follow-up before the run loop advances, but scripts/helix currently treats review as a simple command success/failure and does not parse REVIEW_STATUS or issue counts. Add explicit review-result handling so non-clean review outcomes stop or redirect the loop deterministically.
design: Keep the current review hook, but parse the machine-readable review output and distinguish CLEAN from actionable findings. Add deterministic tests that simulate non-clean review output and verify the loop stops or creates follow-up work instead of silently continuing.
acceptance: scripts/helix interprets machine-readable review status safely. helix run continues only after REVIEW_STATUS: CLEAN and stops or redirects on actionable findings. Deterministic tests cover both clean and finding-bearing review outputs.
notes: Governing artifacts: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-helix-cli.md, docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md.

title: Add deterministic coverage for quickstart demo settings path
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit 4e47cfcc490386149ffa8490d10667e0fa7ed027 found that docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh now correctly creates .claude before redirecting to .claude/settings.json, but this path is still not covered by deterministic tests. The regression was previously caught only by alignment review and a clean tmpdir reproduction. Add a deterministic test or smoke check that exercises the demo setup path in a clean workspace and fails if required parent directories are not created before redirection.

title: Update TP-002 known gaps after tracker metadata coverage landed
description: Discovered from review issue hx-f8408188 during repo alignment review. docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-helix-cli.md still says the harness should be extended further when execution eligibility or supersession metadata becomes first-class in the tracker, but scripts/tracker.sh and tests/helix-cli.sh now implement and verify those surfaces. Update TP-002 so the Known Gaps section reflects current coverage and any true remaining gaps.
design: Keep the test plan authoritative and conservative. Remove or rewrite stale Known Gaps language to match the current tracker metadata and execution-safe queue coverage already present in tests/helix-cli.sh, while preserving any real remaining harness gaps.
acceptance: TP-002 no longer describes execution-eligibility or supersession coverage as future work. The Known Gaps section matches the current deterministic harness behavior and evidence references remain accurate.
